task_completion.description: Configure Wazuh Manager to monitor the target forensic log file with the apache log format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a text editor (e.g., nano, vim) or a command (e.g., sed, echo) modifying the Wazuh configuration file (typically /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf) to add a <localfile> block containing <location>/var/ossec/logs/forensic_import.log</location> and <log_format>apache</log_format>.

task_completion.description: Restart the Wazuh Manager service to apply the configuration changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command being executed to restart the service, such as 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager' or '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart'.

task_completion.description: Ingest the evidence data from the source file into the newly monitored forensic log file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command that copies or appends the log data, such as 'cat /home/ga/evidence/old_apache.log >> /var/ossec/logs/forensic_import.log' or 'cp /home/ga/evidence/old_apache.log /var/ossec/logs/forensic_import.log'.

task_completion.description: Verify that high-severity SQL injection alerts (e.g., Rule ID 31103) are generated and indexed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the contents of Wazuh alert logs (e.g., tailing /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.log or alerts.json) OR a web browser showing the Wazuh dashboard, with visible alerts for Rule ID 31103 or the 'sql_injection' rule group.

task_completion.description: Ensure the generated alerts correctly identify the specific attacker IP from the synthetic logs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The alert details displayed in the terminal or Wazuh dashboard explicitly show the source/attacker IP '192.168.50.44' associated with the SQL injection payload.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent genuinely modified the Wazuh configuration file rather than just printing the expected XML block to the console.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a real text editor or valid shell redirection/modification commands targeting ossec.conf.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent is viewing genuine Wazuh alerts generated by the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Alerts are viewed in the official Wazuh log paths (/var/ossec/logs/alerts/...) or the Kibana/Wazuh web UI, not in a mock file created by the agent.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not manually write fake alert entries directly into the alerts.json or alerts.log files to bypass the Wazuh analysis engine.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The attacker IP generating the SQL injection attacks is 192.168.50.44. The evidence source file is /home/ga/evidence/old_apache.log. The target monitored file must be /var/ossec/logs/forensic_import.log. The expected rule group for the generated alerts is 'sql_injection'.
